source: Google Search Console Page indexing, authenticated browser view

reason_groups.interpretation: This group contains malformed links and old URL paths. The malformed WhatsApp URL confirms at least one on-page link bug: the external `http://wa.me` URL is being resolved as an internal moonn.ru path.
actions: Fix malformed internal links that wrap external URLs, especially WhatsApp and Tilda CDN paths.
Create a 301 redirect map for old .html and legacy transliterated URLs that have a current equivalent.
Return true 404/410 only for pages that are intentionally removed and have no replacement.
After fixes, validate this GSC reason group.

reason_groups.interpretation: The broad `Disallow: /psiholog` rule also blocks any URL beginning with /psiholog, including /psiholog-moskva-online. This may be intentional for legacy duplicate pages, but it is risky if any /psiholog* page should rank.
actions: Decide whether /psiholog and /psiholog-moskva-online are legacy duplicates or active landing pages.
If legacy: redirect them to the canonical consultation page instead of relying only on robots blocking.
If active: narrow the robots.txt rules and ensure canonical/unique metadata are correct.
Do not remove robots rules globally until page intent is classified.

reason_groups.reason: Alternate page with proper canonical tag
reason_groups.interpretation: Google found alternate URLs that correctly point to another canonical URL. The yandex click-id URL is normal; the page*.html URL should be redirected if it is a legacy public path.
actions: Leave parameter URL canonical behavior intact.
Map page35668815.html to the current canonical page if it is a legacy Tilda URL.
No validation priority unless impressions/clicks exist for these alternates.

reason_groups.interpretation: Google knows these URLs but has not crawled/indexed them yet. The sample mixes date/event pages, topical articles, knowledge-base pages, and utility pages. This is the largest indexation backlog.
actions: Split samples into public evergreen pages, event/date pages, utility/test pages, and pages to retire.
For public evergreen pages: unique metadata, H1/H2, internal links from relevant hubs, image alt, schema type, and sitemap inclusion.
For event/date pages: decide whether they are future public events, archives, or test pages.
For utility pages such as /cta: noindex or remove from sitemap unless there is public search value.
After fixing a batch, request indexing for representative URLs instead of validating the whole group blindly.

reason_groups.interpretation: Google crawled these pages but decided not to index. This usually requires improving uniqueness, quality, canonical clarity, internal linking, and removing duplicates such as AMP/tpost variants if they are not canonical.
actions: Check each URL for content depth, duplicate metadata, canonical URL, indexability, and internal links.
Fix or redirect old page*.html and tpost/AMP variants.
Improve article hub pages that should be indexed, especially emotional-intelligence pages.
Noindex/remove pages with no standalone search intent.

immediate_priority_order: Fix malformed 404 links and redirects because they are concrete site defects.
Classify /psiholog* robots-blocked URLs as legacy or active landing pages.
Batch-fix public evergreen pages from Discovered/Crawled groups using contextual SEO templates.
Keep utility/test pages out of the index deliberately.
Validate GSC groups only after representative fixes are live.
